fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection on line 65 is incorrect. The non-overlap condition `end <= s or start >= e` fails to detect conflicts when intervals touch at their boundaries, whereas the correct non-overlap condition requires strict inequalities (`end < s or start > e`).

spec.expr: (end <= s or start >= e) == (end < s or start > e)

constraints.intent: The buggy non-overlap logic should be equivalent to the correct logic which uses strict inequalities for boundary checks.

spec.expr: all((end__1 < s or start__1 > e) for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the function decides to accept the interval, it must not overlap with any existing interval, including at the boundaries.
If the loop continues past an existing slot, it must be because there is truly no overlap, even at the boundaries.
